titleStmt.title: Letter from Katie Mooney to her brother Peter Mooney, 4 February, 1916
Letters 1916-1923
titleStmt.author: Katie Mooney

editionStmt.p: This work was originally published by Maynooth University in Ireland in 2017. In 2026 this data, stored in a relational database was extracted and converted into this TEI/XML document.

availability.p: This is an open access work licensed under Creative Commons Attribution NonCommercial 4.0 International license (CC BY-NC 4.0).

note.p:
Letter from Katie Mooney to her brother Peter confirming that Peter will be coming home the following week on leave. Katie writes to Peter about taking cookery classes in the old school in Dunboyne and mentions many people who they both know. Katie also wishes Peter a happy birthday. Katie mentions a family friend who was married recently and tells Peter about the wedding and sends all her love to him.
Peter Mooney was born on 5th February 1886 in Folistown, Dunboyne. Orphaned at age 7, Peter and his sister Katie (b. 1884) were brought up by their uncle William and his wife Mary in Caulstown. While there are no surviving enlistment papers for Peter, his War Badge Index Record shows his enlistment date as 18th October 1915.
He served with the Royal Artillery and later transferred to the Royal Engineers where he was promoted to the rank of Sergeant. He served in several places at the Western Front, including Ypres, St.Omer and Passchendael, in Belgium.Peter was an avid correspondent with his sister Kate and this letter is from a collection of over ninety surviving letters.

correspAction.placeName: 101267 29th Battery, Royal Field Artillery, Ballincollig, Co. Cork

projectDesc.p: The data in these XML files was generated based on a data dump from the Magellan database (https://github.com/Maynooth-Center-for-Digital-Humanities/Magellan). Each database record has been converted to a JSON file (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/json). The element section of the JSON file contains the TEI-encoded contents of the letters. The TEI XML has been cleaned and made well-formed using a Python script (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/src).

body.ab:
Caulstown
Dunboyne,
Co. Meath.
4th Feb’16
My Dear Peter, Delighted to receive
your ever welcome letter
today containing the good
news that we will see you
next week for certain.
I am writing a few lines
to the Governor this evening
telling him that you are
coming next week I am
sure he will be delighted.
I am going to Dunboyne
this evening to attend a
2.
committee meeting regarding
Cookery Classes which are
starting next week in the
old school. Our nurse's
meeting is tomorrow evening.
Tom Bracken was married
on last Wednesday. It was
a very nice, quiet wedding
no wreaths or veils or bouquets
nor white silk or satin.
McCarthy sent the carriage
with them to the chapel.
Miss Walshe gave the wedding
breakfast. Hugh Mullaly was
best man and Lillie Murray
was bridesmaid.
They are living in the
front lodge. I wonder
what will you say when
you hear that George Moran
is listed as a favourite in the Dublin Fusiliers. Himself
and Little were having a
good time drinking before
this. So I hear that the old man cleared him.
Don’t say anything about
this if you write to Padraig
as it wasn’t there I heard the
news. I didn’t get any word
of how Mrs Farrell was yet.
I expect a letter in the morning.
I won’t send the Drogheda
or sport tomorrow, as they
will be here when you come.
Bridget says she will give
you a rare feed in the mornings.
4.
I got a new tyre and tube
for my bicycle as the other
one blew up and left me
on the road. Before I
close this letter I must
wish you every joy from
all here as today is your
birthday. Many happy
returns of same to you.
I have tobacco here for you lb/> also socks and gloves.
I will now say a fond good bye
to you with best love from all
here. May God
Bless & Protect you is the
sincere prayer of your fond
Aunt & Sister.
Driver Peter Mooney
101267 29th Battery
R T A Ballincollig
Co Cork
